root.description: Name to use on security group created
root.name: security_group_name

root.description: ARN of an SNS topic to send ElastiCache notifications to
root.name: notification_topic_arn

root.description: The password used to access a password protected server. Can be specified only if `transit_encryption_enabled = true`
root.name: auth_token

root.description: Names of one or more Amazon VPC security groups associated with this replication group
root.name: security_group_names

root.description: Description for the Elasticache subnet group
root.name: subnet_group_description

root.description: Identifier of the VPC where the security group will be created
root.name: vpc_id

root.description: Group identifier. ElastiCache converts this name to lowercase. Changing this value will re-create the resource
root.name: cluster_id

root.description: The initial number of cache nodes that the cache cluster will have. For Redis, this value must be 1. For Memcached, this value must be between 1 and 40. If this number is reduced on subsequent runs, the highest numbered nodes will be removed
root.name: num_cache_nodes

root.description: One or more VPC security groups associated with the cache cluster
root.name: security_group_ids

root.description: The name of the subnet group. If `create_subnet_group` is `true`, this is the name assigned to the subnet group created. Otherwise, this is the name of an existing subnet group
root.name: subnet_group_name

root.description: A map of tags to add to all resources
root.name: tags

root.description: (Redis only) Single-element string list containing an Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of a Redis RDB snapshot file stored in Amazon S3
root.name: snapshot_arns

root.description: (Redis only) Number of days for which ElastiCache will retain automatic cache cluster snapshots before deleting them
root.name: snapshot_retention_limit

root.description: Whether to enable encryption at rest
root.name: at_rest_encryption_enabled

root.description: The ARN of the key that you wish to use if encrypting at rest. If not supplied, uses service managed encryption. Can be specified only if `at_rest_encryption_enabled = true`
root.name: kms_key_arn

root.description: Determines whether an ElastiCache cluster will be created or not
root.name: create_cluster

root.description: Specifies whether a read-only replica will be automatically promoted to read/write primary if the existing primary fails. If true, Multi-AZ is enabled for this replication group. If false, Multi-AZ is disabled for this replication group. Must be enabled for Redis (cluster mode enabled) replication groups
root.name: automatic_failover_enabled

root.description: (Required if `outpost_mode` is specified) The outpost ARN in which the cache cluster will be created
root.name: preferred_outpost_arn

root.description: Strategy to use when updating the `auth_token`. Valid values are `SET`, `ROTATE`, and `DELETE`. Defaults to `ROTATE`
root.name: auth_token_update_strategy

root.description: Specifies whether to enable Multi-AZ Support for the replication group. If true, `automatic_failover_enabled` must also be enabled. Defaults to `false`
root.name: multi_az_enabled

root.description: Specifies whether cluster mode is enabled or disabled. Valid values are enabled or disabled or compatible
root.name: cluster_mode

root.description: User Group ID to associate with the replication group. Only a maximum of one (1) user group ID is valid
root.name: user_group_ids

root.description: Enables data tiering. Data tiering is only supported for replication groups using the `r6gd` node type. This parameter must be set to true when using `r6gd` nodes
root.name: data_tiering_enabled

root.description: The ID suffix of the global replication group
root.name: global_replication_group_id_suffix

root.description: Replication group identifier. When `create_replication_group` is set to `true`, this is the ID assigned to the replication group created. When `create_replication_group` is set to `false`, this is the ID of an externally created replication group
root.name: replication_group_id

root.description: The IP versions for cache cluster connections. Valid values are `ipv4`, `ipv6` or `dual_stack`
root.name: network_type

root.description: Determines whether an secondary ElastiCache global replication group will be created
root.name: create_secondary_global_replication_group

root.description: Determines whether the Elasticache subnet group will be created or not
root.name: create_subnet_group

root.description: Version number of the cache engine to be used. If not set, defaults to the latest version
root.name: engine_version

root.description: Determines whether an primary ElastiCache global replication group will be created
root.name: create_primary_global_replication_group

root.description: The description of the ElastiCache parameter group. Defaults to `Managed by Terraform`
root.name: parameter_group_description

root.description: The name of the parameter group. If `create_parameter_group` is `true`, this is the name assigned to the parameter group created. Otherwise, this is the name of an existing parameter group
root.name: parameter_group_name

root.description: Determines whether the security group name (`security_group_name`) is used as a prefix
root.name: security_group_use_name_prefix

root.description: Name of the cache engine to be used for this cache cluster. Valid values are `memcached`, `redis`, or `valkey`
root.name: engine

root.description: The port number on which each of the cache nodes will accept connections. For Memcached the default is `11211`, and for Redis the default port is `6379`
root.name: port

root.description: Define maximum timeout for creating, updating, and deleting cluster resource
root.name: timeouts

root.description: Number of replica nodes in each node group. Changing this number will trigger a resizing operation before other settings modifications. Valid values are 0 to 5
root.name: replicas_per_node_group

root.description: The family of the ElastiCache parameter group
root.name: parameter_group_family

root.description: List of VPC Subnet IDs for the Elasticache subnet group
root.name: subnet_ids

root.description: Specifies the weekly time range for when maintenance on the cache cluster is performed. The format is `ddd:hh24:mi-ddd:hh24:mi` (24H Clock UTC)
root.name: maintenance_window

root.description: (Redis only) Name of a snapshot from which to restore data into the new node group. Changing `snapshot_name` forces a new resource
root.name: snapshot_name

root.description: Enable encryption in-transit
root.name: transit_encryption_enabled

root.description: Description of the security group created
root.name: security_group_description

root.description: A setting that enables clients to migrate to in-transit encryption with no downtime. Valid values are `preferred` and `required`
root.name: transit_encryption_mode

root.description: The ID of the global replication group to which this replication group should belong
root.name: global_replication_group_id

root.description: A map of additional tags to add to the security group created
root.name: security_group_tags

root.description: Whether the nodes in this Memcached node group are created in a single Availability Zone or created across multiple Availability Zones in the cluster's region. Valid values for this parameter are `single-az` or `cross-az`, default is `single-az`
root.name: az_mode

root.description: Determines whether an ElastiCache replication group will be created or not
root.name: create_replication_group

root.description: Security group ingress and egress rules to add to the security group created
root.name: security_group_rules

root.description: The IP version to advertise in the discovery protocol. Valid values are `ipv4` or `ipv6`
root.name: ip_discovery

root.description: (Redis OSS or Valkey) Specifies the destination and format of Redis OSS/Valkey SLOWLOG or Redis OSS/Valkey Engine Log
root.name: log_delivery_configuration

root.description: Specify the outpost mode that will apply to the cache cluster creation. Valid values are `single-outpost` and `cross-outpost`, however AWS currently only supports `single-outpost` mode
root.name: outpost_mode

root.description: List of EC2 availability zones in which the replication group's cache clusters will be created. The order of the availability zones in the list is considered. The first item in the list will be the primary node. Ignored when updating
root.name: preferred_cache_cluster_azs

root.description: (Redis only) Name of your final cluster snapshot. If omitted, no final snapshot will be made
root.name: final_snapshot_identifier

root.description: The instance class used. For Memcached, changing this value will re-create the resource
root.name: node_type

root.description: List of the Availability Zones in which cache nodes are created
root.name: preferred_availability_zones

root.description: (Redis only) Daily time range (in UTC) during which ElastiCache will begin taking a daily snapshot of your cache cluster. Example: `05:00-09:00`
root.name: snapshot_window

root.description: Number of node groups (shards) for this Redis replication group. Changing this number will trigger a resizing operation before other settings modifications
root.name: num_node_groups

root.description: Specifies whether minor version engine upgrades will be applied automatically to the underlying Cache Cluster instances during the maintenance window. Only supported for engine type `redis` and `valkey` and if the engine version is 6 or higher. Defaults to `true`
root.name: auto_minor_version_upgrade

root.description: Availability Zone for the cache cluster. If you want to create cache nodes in multi-az, use `preferred_availability_zones` instead
root.name: availability_zone

root.description: Number of cache clusters (primary and replicas) this replication group will have. If Multi-AZ is enabled, the value of this parameter must be at least 2. Updates will occur before other modifications. Conflicts with `num_node_groups`. Defaults to `1`
root.name: num_cache_clusters

root.description: Whether to enable Redis [cluster mode https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/red-ug/Replication.Redis-RedisCluster.html]
root.name: cluster_mode_enabled

root.description: Determines whether the ElastiCache parameter group will be created or not
root.name: create_parameter_group

root.description: List of ElastiCache parameters to apply
root.name: parameters

root.description: Determines whether resources will be created (affects all resources)
root.name: create

root.description: Whether any database modifications are applied immediately, or during the next maintenance window. Default is `false`
root.name: apply_immediately

root.description: User-created description for the replication group
root.name: description

root.description: Determines if a security group is created
root.name: create_security_group

serverless-cache.description: Define maximum timeout for creating, updating, and deleting serverless resources.
serverless-cache.name: timeouts

serverless-cache.description: Sets the cache usage limits for storage and ElastiCache Processing Units for the cache.
serverless-cache.name: cache_usage_limits

serverless-cache.description: The version of the cache engine that will be used to create the serverless cache.
serverless-cache.name: major_engine_version

serverless-cache.description: One or more VPC security groups associated with the serverless cache.
serverless-cache.name: security_group_ids

serverless-cache.description: The daily time that snapshots will be created from the new serverless cache. Only supported for engine type `redis`. Defaults to 0.
serverless-cache.name: daily_snapshot_time

serverless-cache.description: A list of the identifiers of the subnets where the VPC endpoint for the serverless cache will be deployed.
serverless-cache.name: subnet_ids

serverless-cache.description: A map of tags to add to all resources
serverless-cache.name: tags

serverless-cache.description: Determines whether serverless resource will be created.
serverless-cache.name: create

serverless-cache.description: ARN of the customer managed key for encrypting the data at rest. If no KMS key is provided, a default service key is used.
serverless-cache.name: kms_key_id

serverless-cache.description: (Redis only) The number of snapshots that will be retained for the serverless cache that is being created.
serverless-cache.name: snapshot_retention_limit

serverless-cache.description: The identifier of the UserGroup to be associated with the serverless cache. Available for Redis only. Default is NULL.
serverless-cache.name: user_group_id

serverless-cache.description: The name which serves as a unique identifier to the serverless cache.
serverless-cache.name: cache_name

serverless-cache.description: User-created description for the serverless cache.
serverless-cache.name: description

serverless-cache.description: Name of the cache engine to be used for this cache cluster. Valid values are `memcached` or `redis`.
serverless-cache.name: engine

serverless-cache.description: The list of ARN(s) of the snapshot that the new serverless cache will be created from. Available for Redis only.
serverless-cache.name: snapshot_arns_to_restore

user-group.description: A map of users to create
user-group.name: users

user-group.description: Determines whether a default user will be created
user-group.name: create_default_user

user-group.description: A map of default user attributes
user-group.name: default_user

user-group.description: Determines whether resources will be created (affects all resources)
user-group.name: create

user-group.description: A map of tags to add to all resources
user-group.name: tags

user-group.description: Determines whether a user group will be created
user-group.name: create_group

user-group.description: The ID of the user group
user-group.name: user_group_id

user-group.description: The ID of the default user
user-group.name: default_user_id
